steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to explain WHY nautical miles are used in seafaring navigation. The answer must connect nautical miles to a reason — not just describe what they are.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Nautical miles are a measure of distance equal to one minute of latitude, which is a feature nautical charts use to calculate distances and positions.’: States the correlation but never explains why that makes nautical miles useful for navigation. It restates facts without supplying the causal reason. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Nautical charts use latitude and longitude to measure long distances; these charts are more accurate than flat maps for measuring distances in seafaring navigation because they account for Earth's curvature.’: Compares chart types but never mentions nautical miles or why they are the chosen unit. Off-target. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Since they directly correlate to the coordinates on nautical charts, which take into account Earth's curvature, nautical miles are an efficient way to calculate distances at sea.’: Completes the full chain — nautical miles match chart coordinates → those coordinates account for curvature → therefore nautical miles are efficient for sea navigation. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Using nautical miles for navigation at sea takes Earth's curvature into account, whereas measuring distances with latitude and longitude coordinates does not.’: Directly contradicts the notes, which state that latitude/longitude coordinates DO account for Earth's curvature. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
